territory.primary_region: DC / Arlington / Bethesda corridor + scattered Districts into northern Virginia

primary_resources: Continuity scrip (fiat, pre-Onset Treasury reserves claimed)

goals.description: maintain institutional claim over former US territory
extract healer-levies from Species I kin networks (covert negotiation ongoing)
pull in frontier districts to reduce span of control

current_schemes.description: formal territorial recognition offered to Species I in exchange for skilled-practitioner levies
covert arbitrage of scrip against Bourse copper via House Beltran
internal counter-intelligence against Reeve sympathizers

internal_tensions.description: Reeve's Potomac Irregulars are nominally loyal but operationally independent
civilian shadow-government resents military dominance
district commanders have significant autonomy

known_information: [power] pre-Onset small arms functional; ammunition reloaded via matter-manipulation
[power] heavy weapons non-operational; logistics on horse-cart

secret_information.GM-only: Reeve has quietly assessed loyalty among flag officers; 60% would follow him openly
Alcott has indirect evidence of Reeve's preparations but lacks authority to purge him
scrip arbitrage is a stopgap; without Bourse cooperation, scrip would collapse within months
Fed Continuity has unopened hard intelligence about anomalous entity patterns from pre-Onset classified sources

fed-continuity.narrative_voice: The institution speaks in memoranda and chain-of-command. Orders come down through channels that still use pre-Onset forms — date stamps, classification markings, signature blocks. The fiction of continuity is maintained not because anyone believes the war is ongoing but because the forms are the only thing holding the structure together. Alcott reads every significant cable. Reeve reads the same cables and smiles. The Pentagon smells of kerosene and horse sweat.

territory.primary_region: northern Virginia suburbs (Fairfax, Arlington exurbs, Loudoun)

primary_resources: extraction from Virginia District tributary townships
scavenged pre-Onset military stockpiles at Quantico periphery
smuggling into DC market (against Fed Continuity embargo)

goals.description: replace Alcott as the voice of institutional legitimacy
absorb Fed Continuity military command; keep civilian shell as propaganda

current_schemes.description: quiet loyalty-assessment of Fed Continuity flag officers (60% favorable)
waiting for Alcott political misstep or health failure
small-scale raids on Species I border settlements (plausible deniability)

internal_tensions.description: Reeve's kinetic depth is strong but he has fewer T6+ subordinates than Alcott
some Irregular captains prefer independence over absorption

known_information: Reeve is T8 kinetic, former Marine colonel
[power] T8 kinetic commander (Reeve) + Auratic-T4 command-presence secondary
[power] ~4_000 personnel, mostly former Fed Continuity regulars
[power] Reeve himself is a battlefield multiplier; his presence stabilizes his units

secret_information.GM-only: Reeve has prepared detailed plans for Pentagon seizure; awaits trigger
Species I border incidents are deliberately provocative, seeking Species I overreaction to justify escalation

potomac-irregulars.narrative_voice: Reeve's men still salute. The salutes are correct. They were trained correctly before the Onset and they have not forgotten. What they have forgotten, or have chosen to ignore, is which direction the orders travel. Reeve speaks quietly when he addresses his officers. He does not need to raise his voice. The Auratic field around him does the raising.

territory.primary_region: Peekskill, Cortlandt, and surrounding Hudson Valley towns; ~90,000 initial, expanded to ~420,000 by T+1

primary_resources: Peekskill reactor electrical output (limited but unique)
tribute extracted from northern Westchester demesnes

goals.description: expand demesne north and south of Peekskill
develop a succession protocol (Kostas has no heir)
convert Catskill Throne tribute into something more like alliance

current_schemes.description: quiet research into replacement matter-specialists for post-Kostas era
negotiating formal trade agreements with Yonkers Compact
fortifying reactor perimeter against unspecified threat (possibly Tower Lords or northward Iron Crown expansion)

internal_tensions.description: Kostas is austere and methodical; popular but not warm
his mineralization is advancing; subordinates discuss his longevity
reactor crew has internal disagreements about safe operational margins

known_information: Kostas is T8 matter-energy; former structural engineer
[power] T8 matter-energy founder (Kostas) with Physical-T5 and Perceptual-T3 secondaries
[power] controls only functional electrical grid in mid-Atlantic (~10,000 household equivalents)

secret_information.GM-only: reactor operates on narrower safety margins than Kostas admits publicly
Kostas's mineralization is terminal; probable lifespan 10-15 more years at current rate
Kostas has privately named "The Measurer" (npc-measurer) as provisional successor but has not announced it

bear-house.narrative_voice: The walls of the compound were laid by Kostas personally. The mineralization was not yet on his face when he built them, only his forearm. The walls remember the shape of his hand. Inside the compound, the reactor hums at a frequency that settles into the bones of everyone who lives there. Children born in the Bear-House can feel when the reactor's output changes. They learn to read it before they learn to read words.

territory.primary_region: Yonkers and adjacent Lower Westchester townships

primary_resources: modest salvage from southern Westchester ruins

goals.description: train next generation of biokinetic practitioners

current_schemes.description: negotiating special-status arrangement with Catskill Throne short of formal vassalage
expanding apprenticeship intake to prepare for Three Judges succession
quietly supporting Species I diaspora through healer deployments to Philadelphia

internal_tensions.description: three Judges council has one contested seat (Villanueva is privately contemplating retirement)
younger Compact members want a more formal constitution
tension between neutrality doctrine and moral obligation to intervene in Pine Barrens

known_information: the richest biokinetic agriculture in Hudson Valley
[power] rotating assembly under three biokinetic healers (Obi, Rubinstein, Villanueva)
[power] militia-grade general defense only (~500 trained personnel)
[power] biokinetic agricultural capacity among the highest per-capita in region

secret_information.GM-only: the Three Judges have privately discussed whether neutrality is sustainable if Pine Barrens Expansion fires
Abel the T7 collie Warped (npc-abel) is a formal non-voting advisor; his presence is not widely known

yonkers-compact.narrative_voice: The Compact speaks in meetings. Most mornings in Yonkers begin with a community gathering — the Three Judges available for arbitration, the daily agricultural rota announced, any disputes heard before the day's work. Nothing in the Compact's architecture prevents a warlord. Its resistance is cultural. People are used to the meetings. They expect to be heard. A warlord would have to break several generations of expectation to impose himself.

territory.primary_region: Catskill passes + tributary Hudson Valley polities

primary_resources: tribute from all Hudson Valley polities (~5% of agricultural surplus)

goals.description: convert tribute into formal vassalage (clock advancing)
maintain personal reclusiveness to preserve mystique
eliminate Hollow Choir threat to eastern Catskills

current_schemes.description: ritualizing tribute collection to normalize subordination
negotiating with Warped leaders (Grey-on-Grey wolf, Grey Walker bear)
monitoring Bear-House for signs of independence

internal_tensions.description: no named successor; retinue speculates about Whelan
Preston's reclusiveness creates information vacuum
his breakthrough cost (loss of fear) isolates him

known_information: Preston is T9 kinetic; the most dangerous person in the region
[power] T9 kinetic specialist (Preston) — most formidable individual in region
[power] Marshal Whelan (T7), "The Shadow" (T6 temporal-spatial), Beatrice Amar (T6 auratic), Dr. Huang (T5 biological)

secret_information.GM-only: Preston has met Grey Walker (T9 warped bear) at least twice; he takes the Prior Cycles Theory seriously
Preston's loss of fear extends to loss of self-preservation instinct; he could die from overextension
The Shadow is actually a pair of individuals sharing the title; Preston knows this

catskill-throne.narrative_voice: The Throne receives tribute at the winter and summer gates. Preston does not usually appear. When he does, he stands very still and speaks briefly. The hall is a matter-manipulated addition to a federal bunker — walls rebuilt by a Species C artisan to Preston's specifications, seamless and sound-deadening. Beatrice Amar's truth-field is noticeable within seven meters of her. Whelan conducts most business. Preston watches.

territory.primary_region: Brooklyn districts (Bay Ridge, Flatbush, Crown Heights, etc.)

goals.description: prevent any single Tower Lord from dominating
avoid direct conflict with Iron Crown or Staten Citadel

current_schemes.description: shifting alliances among the 5-6 Lords (perpetual)
quiet succession jockeying (38% 5-year leadership-change probability per Lord)
Manhattan salvage extraction at increasing depth

internal_tensions.description: no stable coordinating mechanism; peer conflicts are constant
individual succession crises are frequent
district loyalty is often weak

known_information: most heavily corrupted terrain in mid-Atlantic
[power] 5-6 T7 warlords, each holding a district
[power] plus 2-3 others, details vary by succession churn
[power] heavy kinetic corruption in district cores

secret_information.GM-only: the number of Tower Lords has varied from 4 to 7 in the past year; formal count is unreliable
at least one Tower Lord is conducting illicit tier-advancement experiments with a Camden contact (possibly npc-doctor-pale)
the Chain-King (npc-chain-king) operates within Tower Lord territory with informal tolerance

tower-lords-brooklyn.narrative_voice: The Lords do not speak to each other often. They speak through intermediaries, messages, arranged meetings at neutral locations in Flatbush or along the waterfront. A salvage crew passing between districts pays each Lord in turn. The crews know the customs. The kinetic corruption has made streets strange — walls sag in directions that would require force-vector analysis to predict. Children born in Crown Heights this year will grow up on streets that are not quite the streets their parents knew.

territory.primary_region: central Queens neighborhoods (Flushing excluded — Species C cluster)

primary_resources: urban agriculture (biokinetic-assisted rooftop and lot farming)
tooling and crafts from Species C adjacent production

goals.description: defend Cross-Queens Line against Tower Lord encroachment
coordinate with Species C Twelve Hands
absorb refugees from Tower Lord territory where possible

current_schemes.description: quiet extension of biokinetic agriculture into former Tower Lord-adjacent lots
formalizing alliance with Flushing-Edison Cluster
negotiating cross-bay trade with Species I Philadelphia kin

internal_tensions.description: Voice network coordination requires perceptual-primary individuals; recruitment is slow
some districts favor a more militant posture

known_information: [power] ~30 T5+ perceptual organizers ("Voices") coordinating across districts without radio
[power] notable alliance with Father Marcus Rhodes' outer Queens community

secret_information.GM-only: the Voice network has been compromised once (month 8 of Year 1) by Tower Lord infiltrator; full security audit ongoing
Jamaica Bay fishery is contested between Commonage and a minor Tower Lord; conflict may escalate

queens-commonage.narrative_voice: The Voices do not speak aloud when they are coordinating. It looks, to an outsider, like neighbors standing too still. In the markets, it looks like one merchant walking past another without acknowledgment. What has passed between them is an update, a warning, or a confirmation. The Commonage runs on this invisible current. It is more fragile than it appears, because perceptual-primary individuals are not common. Every one they lose is not easily replaced.

territory.primary_region: Staten Island entire + Brooklyn Navy Yard sub-claim

primary_resources: harbor tolls (Iron Crown, Tower Lords, occasional Bourse)
the Brooklyn Navy Yard facility (partial operation)

goals.description: develop formal commercial relationship with Bourse

current_schemes.description: preparing for Iron Crown war (clock at 3/6)
discreet recruitment of temporal-spatial manifesters from other polities
fortification of harbor approaches

internal_tensions.description: Chen's succession is unclear (no named heir)
"The Bridgemaster" (npc-bridgemaster) is ambitious
economic dependency on Manhattan salvage creates anomalous-entity risk exposure

known_information: Chen controls harbor via spatial teleportation
extracts tolls from all harbor traffic
preparing for war with Iron Crown
[power] T7 temporal-spatial founder (Chen) with Perceptual-T4 secondary
[power] ~15 T5+ temporal-spatial subordinates (rare specialty; difficult to recruit)
[power] short-range teleportation enables harbor logistics without bridges

secret_information.GM-only: Chen has privately assessed that he will win an open war with Volk within 60 days if it starts
the Brooklyn Navy Yard sub-claim is contested by one Tower Lord; current arrangement is informal and fragile

staten-citadel.narrative_voice: The harbor at Staten is quiet in a way that makes newcomers uneasy. There is no bridge traffic. Goods appear at the docks without the usual human commotion. Chen's temporal-spatial crew works invisibly — crates arrive from Manhattan already on the Staten side, dropped into place by operators who do not need to speak. The fortifications on the eastern shore face Newark. Every week they are a little more substantial. Chen is preparing.

territory.primary_region: Port Newark + Jersey City + Elizabeth

primary_resources: Manhattan salvage partnership with Tower Lords

goals.description: establish dominance over Staten Citadel in Manhattan salvage
expand into northern New Jersey (Paterson, Union)
eliminate perceived Species H threat after T+4-month incident

current_schemes.description: positioning for Iron Crown-Staten war (clock 3/6)
intimidation campaigns against Species I Herrera-kin (Camden)
tightening captain loyalty through selected executions

internal_tensions.description: Volk's personal rule has no succession mechanism
multiple captains have quiet external contacts
Razorgrin (Species H) is anomaly in retinue; loyalty unclear

known_information: Volk is T8 kinetic + dread-field; former longshoreman
Iron Crown chit currency is worthless outside territory
[power] T8 physical primary (Volk) with Auratic dread-field secondary and Biological-T3 tertiary
[power] ~30 T5-T7 captains (Brick, Razorgrin, The Butcher, Silent Henry)
[power] Iron Crown chit currency (fiat, enforced only within territory)

secret_information.GM-only: Brick Stepanski has been approached by Tower Lords for potential alliance; has not yet responded
The Butcher (Petra Voss) has access to unique biological interrogation techniques; Silent Henry trades with her reluctantly
Silent Henry is Volk's de-facto intelligence chief but is also the single best-informed Iron Crown figure about external risks

iron-crown.narrative_voice: Volk's compound at Port Newark smells of harbor tar and iron. The dread extends about thirty meters from him at rest. Visitors pass through it involuntarily, feel their stomach drop, and then stand before him reporting whatever they were sent to report. He listens carefully and speaks briefly. His captains are heavy men with scars who were already heavy men with scars before the Onset. The Iron Crown rules by fear, and fear is a technology that works.

territory.primary_region: Philadelphia Old Market + Delaware wharves + trade networks region-wide

primary_resources: trade arbitrage (grain, salvage, coal, fine goods)
currency issuance (Bourse copper, silver, gold stamps)
arbitration fees and commercial dispute services

goals.description: complete Currency Stability clock (copper as formal regional standard)
contain Hive (burn-crew contracts funded by Grove-coin levy)

current_schemes.description: final push to complete Currency Stability (clock 5/6)
covert backing of Central Jersey League, Species I Reyes-kin, Delmarva Harvest Lords
mediation of Iron Crown-Staten tensions (two failed attempts)
quiet arbitrage of Continuity scrip against copper (House Beltran)

internal_tensions.description: house competition is constant though usually non-violent
South Philly Holding's loyalty is contractual; Marcus Dreng could defect
Factor-General rotation generates succession jockeying every 2 years

known_information: Bourse copper is the most trusted regional currency
backed by South Philly Holding for enforcement
[power] rotating Factor-General (Beltran, T6 perceptual, Species I Herrera-kin)
[power] no standing military; contracts with South Philly Holding (Marcus Dreng, T7 kinetic) for enforcement
[power] Bourse library, printing press, copper coinage operation

secret_information.GM-only: the Currency Stability clock completion is a deliberate multi-year strategy coordinated by House Okonkwo
Bourse library (npc-pilar-reyes) holds pre-Onset intelligence documents smuggled from Federal archives; very sensitive
House Pereira (Camden operations) has unofficial contact with The Broker eldritch entity; exchange of pre-Onset mechanical objects for information

philadelphia-bourse.narrative_voice: The Bourse operates in daylight. Its operations are legible to anyone who cares to read the ledgers. This is a deliberate posture — the Bourse's neutrality is maintained by being obviously profitable for everyone. A merchant house that tried to cheat the arbitration would lose business within a year. The Council of Thirty-One meets weekly in the Old Market's upper gallery. Most business is routine. What is not routine is handled in private meetings between house principals that nobody documents.

territory.primary_region: South Philadelphia + Navy Yard

primary_resources: salvage operation at Philadelphia Navy Yard

goals.description: avoid entanglement in faction wars beyond Philadelphia

current_schemes.description: quiet negotiation with Bourse for expanded enforcement mandate
fortifying Navy Yard perimeter

internal_tensions.description: Dreng's captains question Bourse subordination
navy yard salvage is declining; replacement revenue uncertain

secret_information.GM-only: Dreng has been offered a larger sum by the Crabclaw Confederation to relocate; is considering but has not accepted
Navy Yard salvage runs have produced two anomalous objects in the past year; one was quietly given to Bourse, one disappeared

south-philly-holding.narrative_voice: Dreng is comfortable with Bourse subordination because Bourse pays and because Bourse does not meddle. His men patrol the Delaware wharves, guard Bourse shipments, and break heads when told to break heads. The Navy Yard operation is quieter. Dreng does not like anomalous objects. He prefers to move them up the chain quickly.

territory.primary_region: 15 townships from Princeton to Brick, population ~780,000

goals.description: counterbalance warlord states through soft power
resist Species I pressure on southern townships (or integrate via alliance)

current_schemes.description: quiet absorption of peripheral townships from collapsing Fed Continuity districts
formal Warped diplomacy (Warped relations subcommittee; White Horse of Princeton treaty)
biokinetic training program expansion

internal_tensions.description: Okafor is aging; successor unclear
northern townships (closer to Iron Crown) favor harder line
Accord Council consensus-model is slow under crisis

known_information: [power] 120 T5+ power users across member militias

secret_information.GM-only: Okafor's health is declining; she has been privately treated by Yonkers Three Judges twice in past year
the Warped subcommittee has documented at least 7 verified T6+ Warped contacts in the region (most not publicly known)
Princeton University library holds pre-Onset federal classified materials Okafor has not disclosed to Accord

central-jersey-league.narrative_voice: The League is slow. It is slow by design. The Accord Council meets quarterly; contentious matters take several meetings to resolve. Okafor is the reason it works — her Auratic field is a calming presence, her Perceptual reads the room, her Biological gifts give her concrete gifts to offer townships that align. When she is gone the League will either find another coordinator or it will fragment. The younger practitioners at Rutgers know this.

territory.primary_region: Lehigh Valley coal towns + surrounding Pennsylvania

goals.description: preserve Principality autonomy within loose coordination
resist Hive expansion from Susquehanna Crown

current_schemes.description: seeking Bourse burn-crew contract for Susquehanna Crown containment
quiet arrangement with Old Badger (T8 warped, npc-old-badger) for territorial boundary
expanding Anna Veldt's inter-Principality coordination

internal_tensions.description: Principality lords occasionally feud over coal rights
Species G integration works but is one generation deep

known_information: [power] matter-manipulator-supported coal mining (only functional mid-Atlantic coal)
[power] Species G cluster presence (~168k) integrated as advisors/specialists
[power] Councilor Anna Veldt (Species G) agricultural coordination

secret_information.GM-only: Carbon County coal operation is compromised by undetected Hive root intrusion; discovery may cause a containment crisis
the Old Badger treaty is one of the most stable Warped agreements in region; Veldt manages it personally

lehigh-principalities.narrative_voice: The valleys smell of coal and turned earth. A lord's estate is a manor house, a mine, a biokinetic garden, a stable, a small retinue. Twelve of these, more or less; they coordinate through Allentown but do not obey any central authority. Species G councilors walk the fields with the lords; sometimes they are heard before the lords. Veldt travels between estates most weeks. The industry of this region is the reason it functions. The reason it works is she.

territory.primary_region: Baltimore metro

current_schemes.description: preparing for Fed Continuity war (clock 3/6)
Inner Harbor lord courting Dundalk to prevent Towson alliance
fishing fleet expansion

internal_tensions.description: three lords are uneasy peers; no dominant figure
Fort McHenry defensive line is stretched

known_information: [power] 3 T7 warlords, cooperative but not unified
[power] biokinetic-altered Chesapeake fishery (quarters regional food supply)

secret_information.GM-only: Inner Harbor lord is the most ambitious; has sought alliances against his peers
Fed Continuity intelligence (Sternberg's operation) has multiple informants in the Inner Harbor court

crabclaw-confederation.narrative_voice: Baltimore kept its harbor. The Chesapeake fishery is the reason. The three lords preside over what was once the inner harbor promenade, now a trading quay with biokinetic-altered catch offloaded daily. They rarely meet. When they meet, it is at Fort McHenry, which is a ruin but a neutral ruin. None of them trusts the other two. All three of them know that trust is not what keeps their confederation standing.

territory.primary_region: Delmarva peninsula

goals.description: maintain individual autonomy (Unification clock very slow)
defend against northward threats (Irregulars, Iron Crown)

current_schemes.description: informal Harvest Council as needed
Bourse relationship management
Lord Velasquez (Species I) coordinates with Species I diaspora

internal_tensions.description: no lord will accept subordination to another
Lord Archer's auratic calm-zone moderates conflict but cannot prevent it
Brother Tam's monastic labor-absorption is politically novel

known_information: [power] biokinetic agricultural production most efficient in region

secret_information.GM-only: Lord Marsden's altered-cattle are producing inconsistent offspring; three calves this year were non-viable; may indicate biokinetic breeding limit
Brother Tam's monastery has taken in several orphaned eldritch-adjacent children; result unknown

delmarva-harvest-lords.narrative_voice: The peninsula is green. That is what a visitor from Newark would notice first. Fields stretch to horizons unbroken by ruins. The lords' estates are modest by pre-Onset standards but substantial by current ones. Six lords, six courts, six sets of retainers. A Harvest Council happens when something threatens all six at once. In the interim each lord governs his demesne like a small principality. Lord Archer's lands feel, specifically, different. The calm is noticeable within an hour of arriving.

current_leader.id: distributed; Elder Flores senior, Tomás Reyes ascendant

territory.primary_region: distributed; major concentrations in western Philadelphia, DC/PG County, Wilmington

primary_resources: Species I Flores-kin agriculture in PG County

goals.description: protect kin networks from hostile factions
expand economic integration with friendly polities
prepare for potential Pine Barrens breach (Herrera-kin is proximate)

current_schemes.description: quarterly assemblies processing Reyes' consolidation arguments
negotiating with Fed Continuity for formal territorial recognition
Bourse commercial agreements
kin-to-kin coordination channels

internal_tensions.description: Elder Flores (conservative) vs. Tomás Reyes (pro-consolidation)
Herrera-kin faces Iron Crown pressure and favors consolidation
Diaz-kin (DC) fears Irregular retaliation if consolidation occurs

known_information: [power] 6 major kin networks + smaller family groups
[power] species-typical perceptual boost in all members
[power] Tomás Reyes (T6 perceptual/auratic), Elder Flores (T4 perceptual)
[power] ~400 T5+ perceptual manifesters across kin networks

secret_information.GM-only: Tomás Reyes' kin-polling puts consolidation support at 55% among reproductive-age adults; Elder Flores' generation is 30% support
if Fed Continuity falls to Reeve, Species I Diaz-kin has contingency plans to evacuate DC inner city en masse within weeks

species-i-diaspora.narrative_voice: Species I does not meet in one room because there is no one room it could meet in. The quarterly assemblies are held in Philadelphia, then PG County, then Camden, then Wilmington, rotating. Decisions are reported back to kin-networks through couriers and through the species-typical long-distance recognition. A kin member in Camden knows, within a day, what has been decided in Philadelphia. This works. It also prevents the speed of action that a polity would need. The clock measures how long that limitation remains tolerable.

territory.primary_region: Flushing (Queens) + Sunset Park (Brooklyn) + Edison township (NJ)

primary_resources: fine-craft production (tools, precision components, salvage restoration)

current_schemes.description: expanding apprentice intake (post-Onset-born cohort)
formalizing Bourse commercial relationship
cross-cluster coordination with Species C in other US regions (courier relay)

internal_tensions.description: Edison township Species C is less traditional than Flushing/Sunset Park
some members want to migrate toward stronger polities

known_information: workshops in Flushing, Sunset Park, Edison

secret_information.GM-only: Iwo Kuan has restored one pre-Onset anomalous object (mechanism of unclear function) and stores it privately
the Twelve Hands have been approached by Fed Continuity Reeve faction for weapons-production capability; refused

flushing-edison-cluster.narrative_voice: Master Kuan's workshop is above the Flushing central market. Customers climb a narrow stair and wait in a small anteroom. When he is working, he does not speak; his hands are silvered to the wrist and move with a precision that is not quite craft, not quite manifestation, something between. His apprentices sit in the next room working their own pieces. A pre-Onset wristwatch, restored by him, is worth a month of a retinue soldier's wages.

territory.primary_region: Paterson + Passaic + Union + Bucks County + outer Queens

goals.description: defend enclaves from Iron Crown aggression
rebuild relations with Iron Crown post-boycott
coordinate with Species H primary cluster (limited)

current_schemes.description: courier network optimization across 20+ enclaves
quiet arms accumulation
Bourse commercial relationship expansion

internal_tensions.description: decentralized consensus model is slow under crisis
some enclaves favor more aggressive posture toward Iron Crown
Razorgrin (in Iron Crown service) is a community embarrassment

known_information: courier network known to major factions
[power] Rafael Montes (T4 biological) Bucks County coordinator

secret_information.GM-only: the Sparrow network has compiled surveillance profiles on all major factions; Bourse pays premium for selected excerpts
Rafael Montes has been contacted by Old Black (T8 crow warped) for informal information exchange

north-jersey-hs.narrative_voice: The Hs do not gather in large numbers often. They prefer the township scale. A courier runs the routes week after week, timed to pass through each enclave on predictable days. The coordination happens in these passes — a conversation on a porch, a meal shared, information handed off. The network is invisible to casual observers. This is by design. The Iron Crown learned about it when they tried to seize a workshop and discovered, suddenly, that every enclave in the region had simultaneously cut their medical services.

current_leader.id: npc-anna-veldt (functionally, though not formally polity leader)

territory.primary_region: central NJ (Princeton-New Brunswick) + Lehigh Valley

goals.description: preserve Species G cultural practices (heathen-adjacent revivals)

current_schemes.description: seed exchange expansion
Lehigh-CJL agricultural coordination
Brother Tam monastery collaboration (non-Species-G but similar ethos)

internal_tensions.description: integration is generationally deep but not unanimous
some G members want formal community organization

known_information: most institutionally integrated of the cluster species
[power] integrated into Lehigh Principalities and Central Jersey League
[power] Councilor Anna Veldt (T3 biological, Species G)

secret_information.GM-only: the G community has been testing seed varieties that show unusual biokinetic response; several are not yet released
some G members privately practice heathen-revival religion (distinct from Seven-Day Saints); not publicly acknowledged

lehigh-jersey-gs.narrative_voice: Councilor Veldt travels between Principality estates most weeks. Her manner is measured. She is consulted on seed, on soil, on livestock, on planting schedules, on the arrangement of hedgerows. The Species G community does not meet formally at regional scale; it meets in the fields. Harvest ceremonies in the Lehigh Valley have quietly incorporated older forms — a practice that Lord Meisner of Jim Thorpe has neither encouraged nor discouraged.

current_leader.id: "The One Who Waits" (eldritch sovereign) + human cult hierarchy

territory.primary_region: Pine Barrens interior + expanding periphery

primary_resources: ritualized exchange with Pine Barrens resources

goals.description: breach Delaware line (Pine Barrens Expansion clock at 3/12)
maintain alliance with One Who Waits

current_schemes.description: proselytization through transformed messengers
quiet recruitment in southern NJ townships
territorial incremental expansion
coordination with Sprawl and Cluster

internal_tensions.description: transformed members progress at different rates
cult hierarchy depends on One Who Waits' direction

known_information: Pine Barrens cult centered on One Who Waits
[power] One Who Waits (eldritch sovereign, tier equivalents unclear)
[power] human cult hierarchy with progressive transformation

secret_information.GM-only: One Who Waits has knowledge of pre-Onset events it should not have; one of the anomalous-subset eldritch entities
cult members at later transformation stages cannot return to baseline form; early-stage defectors are possible
the Lost Thing (T10 warped, npc-lost-thing) is treated with deference by The Listening — possibly allied, possibly controlled, possibly neither

the-listening.narrative_voice: The Listening does not speak for itself often. It speaks through messengers. The messengers are visibly wrong. They arrive at a town's edge, deliver a phrase or a demand, and leave. Afterwards the townspeople discover that one or two of them are contemplating joining. The recruitment is patient. It is not coercive in the ordinary sense. It is offered. What is offered is also real — the cult's members do live longer in the Sprawl's shadow, do eat, do have some form of community. The price is the slow unmaking of what they were.

territory.primary_region: same as Yonkers Compact

goals.description: balance biokinetic service provision across Compact and external clients

current_schemes.description: Villanueva retirement deliberation
apprentice expansion
diplomatic outreach to Preston

internal_tensions.description: Villanueva's temporal-sensitivity makes her uniquely valuable; retirement creates succession gap
Obi and Rubinstein privately disagree on external relations strategy

known_information: [power] Sonia Villanueva (T6 Biological/Temporal — rarest combination, sees where healing is needed before injury)

secret_information.GM-only: Villanueva has foreseen her own death in ~4 years; this is affecting her governance decisions
Obi has privately trained Abel (T7 collie warped) as advisor; his status is semi-formalized

the-three-judges-yonkers.narrative_voice: The Judges preside in the Compact's council chamber each morning. Citizens bring disputes. The Judges listen. They speak little and carefully. What they do is mostly heal — their tier means their presence makes people physically better. A tense negotiation, held in the Judges' chamber, softens on its own. Not because the Judges override the parties but because their presence removes one layer of unnecessary resistance.
